Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists CKRC3/TCU2 as AT5G58450.

Source_Species.reason: The study is on Arabidopsis; paper repeatedly states Arabidopsis and Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Title and Methods explicitly name Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper uses Col-0 in Methods for some constructs/mutants, but does not explicitly state CKRC3 source variety for this field as a general gene attribute.

Applied_Species.reason: Genetic and phenotypic analyses were performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods and title specify Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 appears in Methods, but not all applied experiments for CKRC3 are explicitly tied to Col-0 only in the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CKRC3 is linked to maintaining endogenous IAA levels; free IAA was reduced in ckrc3.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IAA is explicitly described as the major natural auxin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states auxin/IAA plays key roles in regulating plant growth and development.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state loss of CKRC3 causes auxin-deficient phenotypes, weaker DR5 reporters, and reduced endogenous free IAA and metabolites.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized points are directly supported in Results: CKRC3 encodes Naa25, ckrc3 is auxin-deficient with reduced IAA, and CKRC3 interacts with NBC/NatB affecting YUC proteins especially YUC8.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all described and used: map-based cloning, allelic analysis, complementation, phenotyping, DR5 reporters, IAA/metabolite measurement, Y2H, Co-IP, and colocalization.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state CKRC3 encodes Naa25, has TPRs in N-terminus and a NatB domain.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results mention cytoplasmic co-localization of CKRC3/TCU2 and NBC.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Conclusions state NatB is widely expressed in tissues throughout plant development, citing Supplementary Fig. 5.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same statement on wide expression throughout plant development is given for Arabidopsis NatB context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Direct interaction with NBC is supported by Y2H and Co-IP, but direct interaction with YUC8 is not shown; CKRC3 affects YUC8 acetylation/stability indirectly via NatB.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 0.1 μM trans-zeatin was used to reveal root curling; exogenous IAA rescued defects.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter is clearly used for some constructs (e.g., Myc-CKRC3, HA-NBC), but saying 'interaction/overexpression assays' broadly for CKRC3 is only partially documented.

Omics_Data.reason: LC-MS/MS was used for N-terminal acetylation analyses, and no public accession is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible implication but is not explicitly presented in the paper as an application value.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results list reduced growth, leaf reticulation, early flowering, aborted ovules, short siliques, impaired apical hooks, and root defects in ckrc3.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper supports that CKRC3 acts post-translationally on YUC proteins and that it was previously named TCU2.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists NBC as AT1G03150.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is used in the study, but NBC source variety is not explicitly specified as a field-level gene attribute.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutant and functional analyses were in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Not all NBC experiments are explicitly tied to a single variety in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: NBC/NatB is linked to maintaining normal IAA levels in Arabidopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Auxin is described as regulating plant growth and development.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state nbc-1 shows similar auxin-deficient phenotypes to ckrc3, and NatB activity affects YUC8 stability/function.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Results support NBC as Naa20 catalytic subunit, interaction with CKRC3 in cytoplasmic NatB complex, and purified NBC acetylating YUC8 N-terminus in vitro.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used and described for NBC: phenotyping, Y2H, Co-IP, localization, in vitro NAT assay, LC-MS/MS.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results identify NBC as Naa20, the catalytic subunit of NatB, i.e., an N-terminal acetyltransferase.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results mention cytoplasmic co-localization of CKRC3/TCU2 and NBC.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Conclusions state NatB is widely expressed in tissues throughout plant development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same wide-expression statement applies in the Arabidopsis NatB context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Interaction with CKRC3/TCU2 is directly shown, but direct physical interaction with YUC8 is not demonstrated; NBC acetylates YUC8 peptide/substrate.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 0.1 μM trans-zeatin was used in phenotypic assays, and exogenous IAA rescue is shown in NatB-related phenotypes.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods/Results state 35S::Myc-CKRC3 and 35S::HA-NBC were used in interaction assays.

Omics_Data.reason: LC-MS/MS was used for peptide acetylation analyses; no public accession is given.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application, not explicitly claimed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper shows nbc-1 has similar auxin-deficient phenotypes, but the exact generalized wording 'pleiotropic growth defects' is not detailed for NBC as extensively as for ckrc3.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper supports NBC as catalytic NatB component acting on biosynthetic enzymes rather than catalyzing the auxin chemical conversion itself.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists CKRC2/YUC8 as AT4G28720.

Enzyme_Name.reason: YUC proteins are described as flavin monooxygenase-like proteins; YUC8 is a YUCCA member.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods say full-length CDS of YUC8 was amplified from Col-0, but the field as a general source-species attribute is not always explicit across experiments.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses of YUC8 were done in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods explicitly mention transgenic lines in Col-7 and YUC8 CDS amplified from Col-0; both Col-0 and Col-7 appear in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Paper states YUCs catalyze the rate-limiting step of auxin biosynthesis via the IPyA pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The IPyA pathway is described as the main auxin biosynthesis pathway.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper states TAA converts Trp to IPyA, followed by YUC conversion of IPyA to IAA, and that YUCs catalyze the rate-limiting step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Paper explicitly describes YUC-catalyzed conversion of IPyA to IAA; enzyme assay used IPyA as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Paper states YUC proteins catalyze conversion of IPyA to IAA.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This reaction description matches the paper's description of YUC function in the IPyA pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Results explicitly say YUCs catalyze the rate-limiting step of auxin biosynthesis via IPyA.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: YUC8 is directly part of the step forming IAA from IPyA.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: IAA is the end product discussed for YUC8 action.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Auxin/IAA regulates plant growth and development per paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show YUC8 overexpression causes high-auxin phenotypes in WT, while NatB deficiency destabilizes YUC8 and impairs its function.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four points are supported: yuc8 mutant had ckrc phenotype, YUC8 is NatB substrate in vitro/in vivo, NatB affects protein abundance not transcript, and E2A reduces abundance/phenotypes while retaining in vitro activity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described and used: mutant phenotyping, overexpression, qRT-PCR, WB, transient protoplast assay, enzyme assay, NAT assay, in vivo LC-MS/MS, rescue in yuc8.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper describes YUCs as flavin monooxygenase-like proteins.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper does not demonstrate direct physical interaction of YUC8 with CKRC3/TCU2 or NBC; it shows YUC8 is a NatB substrate.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 0.1 μM trans-zeatin is used in phenotypic assays; long-day conditions are described in Methods.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods and Results explicitly mention 35S::YUC8-mGFP and related constructs.

Omics_Data.reason: LC-MS/MS is used for in vitro and in vivo NTA detection; HPLC is used in YUC8 enzyme assay; no public accession is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication but not explicitly presented in the paper as an engineering recommendation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results explicitly state YUC8 overexpression in WT produced high auxin phenotypes including long hypocotyl and epinastic cotyledons.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Conclusions and earlier Results state 8 of 11 Arabidopsis YUCs possess NatB substrate signatures, suggesting broader NatB control beyond YUC8.

corrections.reason: The paper does not demonstrate direct physical interaction of YUC8 with CKRC3/TCU2 or NBC; it shows YUC8 is a NatB substrate.
